commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7652fa3654e242a8c7c2f860ec98dc06659e77c6)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a WhatsApp `readOnly` account config with docs/schema updates, listener send and presence guards, drop-result plumbing, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +255, Tests +54, Docs +43, Generated 0. Total +352 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: the PR source returns a read-only drop with `messageId: "dropped"`, then `sendMessageWhatsApp` returns that id to callers. Current approval and outbound adapter callers treat any truthy message id as delivered.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused repair can preserve the contributor's read-only mode while fixing the new outbound result contract; maintainers still own the product decision for the new config mode.

target.title: feat(whatsapp): add readOnly account mode
target.body: ## Summary What problem does this PR solve? - WhatsApp accounts had no way to operate in a purely passive / monitoring mode — any triggered session may send replies to the chat, with no deterministic mechanism to suppress all outbound writes. Why does this matter now? - Enables non-intrusive monitoring, archival, and ambient-awareness use cases (e.g. connecting an account to observe a group without the risk of the agent responding). What is the intended outcome? Setting `readOnly: true` on an account puts it into passive observation mode. OpenClaw receives and processes inbound messages normally but never writes anything back to WhatsApp on that account: - **Outbound messages are always suppressed** — no message, poll, or reaction will ever be sent, regardless of trigger (agent reply, agent initiative, or explicit owner request). - **Typing indicators are suppressed** — neither the connect-time presence announcement nor per-turn composing presence is sent. - **Owner slash commands are silently dropped** — commands like `/reset` are interactive and confirm via a reply; on a read-only account that reply can never be sent, so the command is dropped before processing. - **Read receipts are independent** — governed by `sendReadReceipts` separately from `readOnly`. Typical use: a personal number used to read and summarize conversations without ever replying on your behalf; or a compliance / archive account that must not send under any circumstances. Feature documentation was added to the WhatsApp channel docs. Developed with the help of github co-pilot using Claude Sonnet 4.6 What is intentionally out of scope? - No runtime toggle — changing `readOnly` requires a gateway restart (same lifecycle as all account-level config). - No new agent tool to set `readOnly`. By design, `readOnly` is not in the `assertGatewayConfigMutationAllowed` allowlist, so agents cannot mutate it. - No UI surface — config only. What does success look like? - Agent sessions run normally (`prompt.submitted` → `model.completed`), tools are called, model generates replies — but nothing reaches the WhatsApp socket when `readOnly: true`. Gateway log shows `Dropped X (read-only)` lines at `info`, no `Sent X` lines. Removing the flag restores normal behavior with `Sent X` confirmations. What should reviewers focus on? - Guard coverage across all outbound paths: `send-api.ts` (`sendMessage`, `sendPoll`, `sendReaction`), `monitor.ts` (connect presence, pre-dispatch gate), `deliver-reply.ts` (auto-reply path), `send.ts` (`sendMessageWhatsApp`, `sendReactionWhatsApp`, `sendPollWhatsApp`). - The `dropReason` propagation chain added to `WhatsAppReplyDeliveryResult` in `deliver-reply.ts`, and whether the unified `normalizeWhatsAppSendResultDropped` helper in `send-result.ts` is the right abstraction boundary. ## Linked context Which issue does this close? Closes #78963 (partial — this PR covers the passive / silent-account part of the request). Other requests in that issue not involving the LLM can be addressed in separate PRs. Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related? Related # Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? Owner implementation. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - **Behavior addressed:** WhatsApp account silences all outbound sends when `readOnly: true`; gateway log confirms drops at `info` with `dropReason: "read-only"`; normal sends still confirmed when `readOnly: false`. - **Real environment tested:** - macOS - Model: `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview` - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Toggled `readOnly` in `openclaw.json`, restarted gateway, triggered agent turns from WhatsApp group or direct channel. - **Evidence after fix:** Screenshots and Gateway log excerpts - **Observed result after fix:** All live test areas passed. - **What was not tested:** Poll sending (`sendPollWhatsApp`) — no live test; covered by code inspection and same guard/log pattern as reactions. The implicit auto-reply pipeline (`reply()` / `sendMedia()` closures in `inbound/monitor.ts`) was not live-tested — the model always uses the explicit `message` tool; covered by unit tests in `deliver-reply.test.ts` and `monitor-inbox.read-only.test-support.ts`. - **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** `readOnly: true` composing suppression has no app-level log; behavioral confirmation only (user observed no typing indicator on device. Configuration used in the tests: ```json "channels": { "whatsapp": { "enabled": true, "accounts": { "test": { "readOnly": false, // toggeled "groupPolicy": "allowlist", "groupAllowFrom": [ "*" ], "groups": { "120363406415684625@g.us": { "requireMention": false, // modified "systemPrompt": "Be nice!" } }, "debounceMs": 0 } } } } ``` **Manual test summary** | # | Area | Passes run | Outcome | | --- | ------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | Text reply | `readOnly=false` · `readOnly=true` | ✅ Both passes | | 2 | Media reply | `readOnly=false` · `readOnly=true` | ✅ Both passes | | 3 | Composing indicator | `readOnly=false` · `readOnly=true` | ✅ Both passes (behavioral; no app-level log) | | 4 | Reaction sending | `readOnly=false` · `readOnly=true` | ✅ Both passes | | 5 | Control command drop | `readOnly=false` · `readOnly=true` | ✅ Both passes | | 6 | DM access-control pairing reply | `readOnly=true` | ✅ Pairing reply suppressed — `sendTrackedMessage` catch-all guard exercised | ** Screen Capture — Text reply readOnly=false ** **Gateway log — Text reply, `readOnly=false` (runId `e28bd02c`, 13:23:54–13:24:16 IDT)** ``` 13:24:15+03:00 info [whatsapp/outbound] Sending message -> sha256:6b385642ffdf 13:24:16+03:00 info [whatsapp/outbound] Sent message 3EB09665DA1CC2E82D9044 -> sha256:6b385642ffdf (707ms) 13:24:16+03:00 info [whatsapp] Marked message 3BA2E1AC2E704DF5CB78 as read for …@g.us ``` → Real WA message ID, 707 ms round-trip confirms socket write reached server. ** Screen Capture — Text reply readOnly=true ** **Gateway log — Text reply, `readOnly=true` (runId `a99f6078`, 13:26:35–13:26:47 IDT)** ``` 13:26:46+03:00 info [whatsapp/outbound] Sending message -> sha256:6b385642ffdf 13:26:46+03:00 info [whatsapp/outbound] Dropped message (read-only) -> sha256:6b385642ffdf (7ms) 13:26:46+03:00 info [whatsapp] Marked message 3B85E9587E30332F0A12 as read for …@g.us ``` → 7 ms vs 707 ms confirms no socket write. No `Sent message` line. Read receipts unaffected. ** Screen Capture — Media reply readOnly=false ** ** Screen Capture — Media reply readOnly=true ** ** Screen Capture — Reaction sending readOnly=false ** ** Screen Capture — Reaction sending readOnly=true ** **Gateway log — Reaction sending, `readOnly=true` (runId `8b8da980`, 15:32:40–15:32:54 IDT)** ``` 15:31:40+03:00 info [whatsapp/native-approvals] disabled ← readOnly=true active on connect 15:32:51+03:00 info [whatsapp/outbound] Sending reaction "👍" -> message 3B848B156E929A0F2761 15:32:51+03:00 info [whatsapp/outbound] Dropped reaction (read-only) "👍" -> message 3B848B156E929A0F2761 ``` → `Dropped reaction (read-only)` log; no reaction appeared on device. ** Screen Capture — Control command readOnly=false ** ** Screen Capture — Control command readOnly=true ** **Gateway log — Control command (`/reset`), `readOnly=true` (13:44:01 IDT)** ``` 13:44:01+03:00 info [web-inbound] inbound message 13:44:01+03:00 info [whatsapp] Marked message 3B5EF502BEE11DC7D983 as read for …@g.us 13:44:43+03:00 info [web-heartbeat] web gateway heartbeat ← idle ``` → No `inbound web message`, no `session turn created`, no send attempt. Guard fires before `onMessage()` — silent drop by design. ** Screen Capture — Typing indication readOnly=false ** Typing indication: Response: ** Screen Capture — Typing indication readOnly=true ** No typing indication No response, read receipt ## Tests and validation Which commands did you run? ``` pnpm test extensions/whatsapp # 856 tests, 82 files — all passing pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts pnpm check pnpm check:test-types ``` What regression coverage was added or updated? - `whatsapp-web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts` — new E2E test covering `readOnly` drop logging for the auto-reply path. - `deliver-reply.test.ts` — new unit tests for `dropReason` propagation in `WhatsAppReplyDeliveryResult`. - `monitor-inbox.read-only.test-support.ts` — shared test-support fixture for `readOnly` inbound scenarios. What failed before this fix, if known? N/A — new feature, not a bugfix PR. If no test was added, why not? N/A — tests were added. ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes — new behavior when `readOnly: true` is set: the account goes silent. No change for existing accounts (flag is optional, absence is equivalent to `false` / current behavior). Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes — new optional `readOnly` boolean field on WhatsApp account config in `openclaw.json`. Defaults to `false` (existing behavior preserved). No migration needed; no existing config files are affected.
